channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: McDonald's rolls back diversity goals
item.description: Fast food giant McDonald’s has become the latest company to roll back diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) commitments following the Supreme Court’s 2023 decision ending affirmative action in college admissions.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KRON4

item.title: Southern US shivers as new winter storm threatens snow for Texas, Oklahoma and Arkansas
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — A developing winter storm threatens to drop snow, sleet and freezing rain on parts of Texas, Oklahoma and Arkansas as frigid air that escaped the Arctic plunges temperatures to subfreezing levels in some of the southernmost points of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter's woodworking, painting and poetry reveal an introspective Renaissance Man
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — The world knew Jimmy Carter as a president and humanitarian, but he also was a woodworker, painter and poet, creating a body of artistic work that reflects deeply personal views of the global community — and himself.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter continues to lie in state at Capitol Rotunda ahead of his state funeral
item.description: Former U.S. president Jimmy Carter will continue to lie in state after his remains arrived in Washington a day earlier as part of state funeral rites.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A second wind-whipped wildfire is burning out of control in the Los Angeles area
item.description: A fast-moving wildfire has broken out in the inland foothills northeast of Los Angeles hours after another blaze tore through the city’s Pacific Palisades neighborhood along the coast, destroying many homes and prompting evacuation orders for tens of thousands.
item.dc:creator: Jaimie Ding, Christopher Weber And Julie Watson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: California governor says Pacific Palisades wildfire has destroyed many structures as winds kick up
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A wildfire whipped up by extreme winds swept through a Los Angeles hillside dotted with celebrity residences Tuesday, burning homes in Pacific Palisades and prompting evacuation orders for tens of thousands.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JANIE HAR and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington on Tuesday for state funeral rites that featured the kind of bipartisan praise and ceremonial pomp the Georgia Democrat rarely enjoyed at his political peak.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, JONATHAN J. COOPER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fierce overnight winds expected to fuel Pacific Palisades fire
item.description: “We looked across and the fire had jumped from one side of the road to the other side of the road,” Resident Kelsey Trainor said. “People were getting out of the cars with their dogs and babies and bags, they were crying and screaming. The road was just blocked, like full-on blocked for an hour.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cybertruck driver used ChatGPT to plan Las Vegas attack, police say
item.description: Police found a 6-page manifesto on Matthew Livelsberger’s phone and say he used ChatGPT to plan his New Year's Day bombing at the front of the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas, the LVMPD said at a news conference Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, KRON4

item.title: In surprising move, former longtime Illinois House speaker takes stand at his own corruption trial
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Once the longest-serving legislative leader in U.S. history, Michael Madigan took the stand Tuesday at his own corruption trial, a shocking move for a man once considered the most powerful politician in Illinois.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man who exploded Tesla Cybertruck outside Trump hotel in Las Vegas used generative AI, police say
item.description: The highly decorated soldier who exploded a Tesla Cybertruck outside the Trump hotel in Las Vegas used generative AI including ChatGPT to help plan the attack, Las Vegas police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wildfire burns through LA enclave known for its beaches and celebrity allure
item.description: The affluent Pacific Palisades neighborhood in Los Angeles has long been a celebrity hideaway and magnet for those drawn to nature’s doorstep not far from the skyline of downtown.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Migrant group being held near Acapulco by Mexican officials
item.description: Mexican government officials are breaking up migrant caravans headed for the U.S. border and sending them elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Schools around the US are weighing responses to possible immigration raids
item.description: Schools around the country are reviewing what to do if immigration agents come knocking as President-elect Donald Trump prepares to take office after campaigning on a pledge to deport millions of people.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Actor Steve Guttenberg jumps into action as brush fire burns in Pacific Palisades
item.description: The "Police Academy" actor was among those working to move abandoned vehicles blocking emergency crews responding to the blaze.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Aurora mayor blasts Denver counterpart for city's Tren de Aragua and migrant crisis
item.description: Aurora Mayor Mike Coffman has called on Denver Mayor Mike Johnston to come clean about his potential role in how two nonprofits allegedly housed migrants in Aurora without notifying local officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oregon places new rules on homeowners living in certain high-risk wildfire areas
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — Oregon homeowners living in certain areas at high risk of wildfire will face stricter building codes and mandates to reduce vegetation on their properties under new “wildfire hazard maps” unveiled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Daniel Penny demands dismissal of civil lawsuit from Jordan Neely's father
item.description: Daniel Penny's lawyers want a judge to dismiss the lawsuit filed by Jordan Neely's father, after their client was acquitted of homicide in criminal court.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York appeals court denies halting Trump's sentencing
item.description: A New York appellate judge on Tuesday declined to suspend President-elect Trump’s Friday sentencing on his hush money criminal conviction.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Payments go out in $100M Verizon settlement, but people are disappointed
item.description: Payments have finally started going out in the $100 million class action lawsuit Verizon agreed to settle, but the deposits are falling short of people's expectations.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Peter Yarrow of Peter, Paul and Mary dies at 86
item.description: Yarrow, who also co-wrote the group's most enduring song, “Puff the Magic Dragon,” died Tuesday in New York.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday refused to commit to not using the U.S. military to gain control of the Panama Canal, after vowing last month to take over the operation of the key passageway.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New labels to help identify devices less at risk of hacking
item.description: The federal government is rolling out a consumer labeling system designed to help Americans pick smart devices that are less vulnerable to hacking.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Body of 'survivalist' photographer missing for weeks discovered near Oregon coast
item.description: The body of Joseph Neill, a photographer who disappeared along the Oregon coast three weeks ago, has been found, authorities said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Orleans attack came at a time of political heat, and transition, for the FBI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Joining law enforcement officials to update the public in the hours after a man killed 14 people and injured dozens of others in New Orleans, Louisiana's junior Republican senator, John Kennedy, implored the FBI to “catch these people" — and then added one more eyebrow-raising requ
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: U-Haul stolen in Albuquerque with Nine Inch Nails memorabilia inside
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (KRQE) – It was not the trip one man driving through Albuquerque expected: his U-Haul full of priceless items from one of his favorite bands was stolen. Ryan Jonik said he's out more than $50,000 from the items stolen, not to mention the sentimental value.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, NewsNation

item.title: Tampa Bay doctor sexually exploited kids, told them to 'hang themselves': DOJ
item.description: A palliative care doctor in Tampa Bay has been arrested after allegedly sexually exploiting several children and directing them to "cut, choke, and hang themselves," according to the U.S. Department of Justice.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, NewsNation

item.title: Pence: 'Admirable' that Harris certified Trump's win after losing election
item.description: The drama-free proceeding stood in stark contrast to four years ago when pro-Trump rioters violently stormed the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ weighs 200 outstanding Capitol riot cases referred by FBI
item.description: Federal prosecutors are mulling bringing charges against as many as 200 more people for their roles in the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol attack, including some 60 individuals suspected of assaulting or impeding law enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Winter weather can disrupt. Here's what to do if your flight is canceled
item.description: Winter weather often causes flight delays and cancellations. While airlines can’t control the weather, they are required in the U.S. to provide refunds to customers whose flights are canceled.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lawsuit alleges ex-Fox Sports host Skip Bayless harassed hairstylist, offered her $1.5 million for sex
item.description: The complaint claims Fox executives fostered a hostile work environment that allowed senior managers and on-air personalities including Bayless to abuse workers without fear of punishment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Detachment risk prompts recall of 145K trailer hitch locks
item.description: The recall says the vehicle may become detached from whatever it's towing due to the potentially loose part on the hitch locks.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, ABC 4

item.title: Jaguars return home after getting stuck on Indianapolis tarmac for 7 hours during storm
item.description: The Jacksonville Jaguars returned home early Monday after spending seven hours waiting through a winter storm on the tarmac at Indianapolis International Airport.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The FBI says the man responsible for New Year's truck attack visited New Orleans twice before
item.description: Shamsud-Din Jabbar, a U.S. citizen from Houston, also traveled to Cairo, Egypt, as well as Ontario, Canada, before the attack.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tech law in 2025: a look ahead at AI, privacy and social media regulation under the new Trump administration
item.description: Artificial intelligence harms, problematic social media content, data privacy violations – the issues are the same, but the policymakers and regulators who deal with them are about to change.
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Lu, University of Michigan, Fox 5

item.title: What's on the table in 2025? Some predicted trends in food
item.description: As we tip into the New Year, we food writers are often tasked with putting on paper our predictions for what will be trending.
item.dc:creator: Katie Workman, WFLA, NBC
